news.title: News & Events
news.desc: Stay up to date with the latest from the Parents' Association.

agm.title: Annual General Meeting 2025
agm.desc: Invitation to the ordinary general meeting of the Parents' Association.
agm.body:
We cordially invite you to the Annual General Meeting of the Parents' Association of HTL Mödling. The meeting will take place on October 15, 2025, at 6:00 PM in the assembly hall of HTL Mödling.

The agenda includes the board's annual report, the treasurer's report with discharge of the board, the election of association officers, and the determination of the membership fee for the upcoming school year.

Your participation is especially important, as essential decisions for the 2025/26 school year will be made at this meeting. A proxy vote is possible in case of absence. Please register by October 10, 2025, at the latest.

sga.title: SGA Meeting Minutes
sga.desc: The minutes of the last School Community Committee meeting are now online.
sga.body:
The minutes of the latest School Community Committee (SGA) meeting are now available for download. Important topics concerning school operations were discussed during the session.

Key resolutions include: approval of the budget for school events in the current school year, updates to the school rules regarding mobile phone usage, and planning for upcoming parents' evenings and information events.

All parents are invited to review the full minutes and to contact the Parents' Association with any questions. The next SGA meeting is scheduled for December 2025.

funding.title: New Funding Opportunities
funding.desc: Applications for project funding can now be submitted digitally.
funding.body:
Funding applications for innovative student projects can now be submitted digitally through our new online portal. The Parents' Association has increased the budget for project funding for the 2025/26 school year.

Projects from all departments of HTL Mödling are eligible for funding – from Information Technology and Mechanical Engineering to Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, and Chemistry. We especially welcome cross-departmental collaborative projects.

Applications are reviewed by the Parents' Association board and processed within three weeks. Upon approval, funds are made available promptly. All information about the application process can be found on our projects page.

parentsDay.title: Parents' Day
parentsDay.desc: Information about the upcoming Parents' Day and appointment booking.
parentsDay.body:
The upcoming Parents' Day will take place on December 10, 2025, at HTL Mödling. Take this opportunity to discuss your children's current academic progress in personal conversations with their teachers.

Appointment booking for the individual meetings is handled through the school portal. Please book your preferred time slots early, as they tend to fill up quickly. Each meeting lasts approximately ten minutes.

The Parents' Association will be present with an information stand. We are happy to answer your questions about our support services, funding opportunities, and upcoming events. Coffee and refreshments will be available.

festival.title: Excursion to Company Fantasie
festival.desc: An exciting company visit offering hands-on insights for students and parents.
festival.body:
On October 15, 2026, the Parents' Association is organizing a company excursion to Company Fantasie. Students from all departments and their parents are cordially invited to gain hands-on insights into industrial practice for a day.

Company Fantasie is a leading technology enterprise in the region, offering exclusive tours of its production facilities, interactive workshops, and networking opportunities with professionals. Catering will be provided on site.

Registration is available through the Parents' Association's internal portal. Places are limited, so early registration is recommended. Bus transport from HTL Mödling will be organized by the Parents' Association and is free of charge for all participants.

jhv.title: AGM Minutes 2025
jhv.desc: Minutes from the last Annual General Meeting. Important voting results.
jhv.body:
The complete minutes of the Annual General Meeting held on September 22, 2025, are now available for download by all members.

In the votes, the annual report was unanimously accepted, the membership fee for the 2025/26 school year was confirmed, and the board was discharged. The board elections resulted in the appointment of three new co-opted members.

The board thanks all attending members for the constructive discussion and lively interest. The next minutes will be published after the autumn session.

mentalHealth.title: Open House Day 2026
mentalHealth.desc: Visit our workshops and laboratories on Open House Day.
mentalHealth.body:
On Open House Day 2026, HTL Mödling opens its doors to all interested visitors. Explore our workshops, laboratories, and classrooms, and experience the diversity of our technical education firsthand.

The Parents' Association will be present with its own information stand and is available to answer questions about school support, funding opportunities, and association activities. Additionally, interactive workshops will be offered for children and young people.

We look forward to your visit and to introducing you to the opportunities at HTL Mödling. The school canteen, supported by the Parents' Association, will provide refreshments.

impressum.purposeDesc: Safeguarding the interests of the association partners regarding teaching and educational work at the school as well as promoting cooperation between home and school.

privacy.intro: We take the protection of your personal data very seriously. Below we inform you about the processing of your data.
privacy.collectionDesc: We process personal data that we receive as part of the membership or through your contact.
privacy.rightsDesc: You generally have the rights to information, correction, deletion, restriction, data portability, revocation and objection.

home.heroDesc: The Elternverein is dedicated to supporting the school community through active participation, funding, and open communication.
home.servicesSub: Innovation meets community – Discover our benefits.
home.heroTitleFluid: Enabling the future together – for our children at HTL Mödling.
home.heroSubtitleFluid: From project grants to social support – we are the strong network behind the students.

socialProof.title: Voices from our Community
socialProof.s1: "Thanks to the parents' association, our son was able to implement his diploma project."
socialProof.s2: "The rapid support helped us a lot."

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
faq.subtitle: Here you will find answers to the most important questions about the parents' association.
faq.q1: Can I help even without a board function?
faq.a1: Yes, absolutely! We welcome every helping hand at our events or projects, without the need for a formal role on the board.
faq.q2: How high is the membership fee?
faq.a2: The membership fee is determined annually at the general assembly and directly benefits the students.
faq.a3: All parents of students at HTL Mödling can apply for support in cases of social hardship or for innovative student projects.
faq.a4: As a non-profit association, we are subject to strict audits. The use of funds is monitored annually by auditors and disclosed at the general assembly.

showcase1.title: Equipment for the Future
showcase1.desc: With parents' contributions, we finance state-of-the-art equipment, lab materials, and projects that otherwise have no place in the regular school budget.

showcase2.title:
Strengthen Community.
Show Presence.
showcase2.desc: In addition to financial support, we organize excursions, strengthen social cohesion on school trips, and actively inform about current concerns in presentations and meetings.

about.title: About the Elternverein
about.desc: The Parents' Association is a voluntary organization of parents and guardians dedicated to promoting the welfare of the students and the school.
about.missionDesc: To foster a close relationship between home and school, so that parents and teachers may cooperate intelligently in the education of their children.
points: Represent parents' interests in school committees
Facilitate communication between parents and school

costs.title: Membership & Costs
costs.details: The membership fee is used 100% for the benefit of the students.
costs.paymentNote: The data are placeholders. Please contact the board for current payment information.

contact.desc: Have questions or want to get involved? Reach out to us directly or fill out the form.

form.name: Name

services.title: Our Services
services.desc: We support students and families with financial aid, tutoring programs, and educational courses.
services.supportDesc: Financial assistance for school events and materials.

socialSupport.intro: A central concern of the Parents' Association is to enable students from financially disadvantaged families to participate in school events. We help where state subsidies are insufficient or arrive too late.
points: Grant for school weeks (sports & project weeks)
socialSupport.whoDesc: All legal guardians of students at HTL Mödling who are members of the Parents' Association can apply. Support is provided discreetly and unbureaucratically after verification of social need.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.desc: Send us a request via the contact form or email.

steps.title: Review
steps.desc: The board will review your application promptly and discreetly.

steps.title: Payment
steps.desc: Upon approval, the amount will be transferred directly to the school or organizer.

socialSupport.qDesc: We are happy to advise you without obligation and support you with the application process.

tutoringPage.subtitle: By students for students
tutoringPage.intro: Nobody knows the pitfalls of an exam better than someone who has already passed it. Our peer-to-peer tutoring system pairs motivated upper-school students with students who need support in specific subjects.
findSteps: Check the current notice board in the hall.
Use our contact form for mediation.
tutoringPage.ctaTitle: Want to become a tutor yourself?
tutoringPage.ctaDesc: Help your classmates, deepen your own knowledge, and earn some extra money. We support you with the mediation.
tutoringPage.noteDesc: Tutoring takes place on a private basis between students. The Parents' Association only provides the platform and contacts. We recommend fair fees between €10 and €15 per hour.

coursesPage.subtitle: More than just teaching
coursesPage.titleHighlight: Additional qualifications for a head start.
coursesPage.intro: We support the acquisition of internationally recognized certificates and special training that go beyond the regular curriculum. This strengthens the job prospects of our graduates.

items.title: ECDL Certification
items.desc: The European Computer Driving Licence for all grades.

items.title: Injection Molding Tech
items.desc: Special training for mechanical and plastics engineering.

items.title: Safety Training
items.desc: Additional qualification for upper-school workshop operations.

points: Cost-effective access through the Parents' Association
